name: Hunan Vegetable Pie B1
recipeInstructions:
--------------------------GARNISH--------------------------
Scallion brushes
Parsley
Decoratively cut radishes
-------------------------PANCAKES-------------------------
2 cups All-purpose flour, sifted
3/4 cup Boiling water
2 tbsp Sesame or cooking oil
---------------------------SAUCE---------------------------
1/2 cup Hoisin sauce
1 tbsp Sesame oil
1 tbsp Sugar
1 tsp Sherry
---------------------SCALLION BRUSHES---------------------
1 bunch Scallions
Ice water
Using wet, hot towels, moisten the bean curd skin sheets and set aside until ready to use. Cover with hot towels to retain moisture. In separate small bowls, cover the dried shrimp and mushrooms with a little boiling water. When soft, drain and mince finely using a knife or cleaver. Mince the ginger.
Finely chop scallions. Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a pan and stir-fry the minced shrimp, mushrooms, and bamboo shoot. Add 1 tablespoon of soy sauce, sherry, sugar, flavor enhancer (optional), and black pepper to taste. Stir for about a minute until most liquid has evaporated. Add scallions and let the filling cool.
In a large, flat, rectangular pan, combine chicken stock and remaining soy sauce. Dip a softened bean curd sheet into the stock mixture and lay it on a work surface. Sprinkle lightly with filling. Repeat with a second bean curd sheet, laying it on top of the first and sprinkling it with a little filling. Cover with a third sheet dipped in the stock but do not sprinkle with filling. Ensure all corners are thoroughly moistened and gently fold into a 7x5-inch shape. Set aside.
Make batter by combining eggs with a little water and beating. Stirring vigorously, pour egg mixture into flour. The resulting batter should be like heavy cream. Add more water if necessary. Heat oil for deep-frying in a wok to 300°F.
Moisten the pie with the egg batter and gently slip it into the hot oil. Increase heat and cook, turning occasionally and gently until golden brown on both sides. If air pockets form during cooking, prick them with a sharp pin. Drain well and cut into 8-10 rectangular pieces. Serve wrapped in a pancake with sauce and scallion brushes garnished with parsley and decorative radishes.
Pancakes: Pour boiling water into flour and mix well with chopsticks. Form dough into a ball and knead on a floured surface for about 10 minutes until smooth and silky. Cover with damp cloth and let rest 15 minutes. After resting, roll dough into a cylindrical shape about 1-1/2 inches in diameter. Cut crosswise into 12 rounds.
Lightly brush oil on one side of each piece. Place two pieces together with oiled sides facing inward, resembling a sandwich. Roll each sandwich into a 6-7-inch circle. Cook the circles one at a time on an ungreased skillet over low heat for 1 minute, then flip and cook until lightly browned. Carefully separate into individual pancakes. Serve immediately or reheat by heating over small quantity of water.
Sauce: Combine all ingredients and serve as sauce for vegetable pie or Peking Duck.
Scallion brushes: Trim root ends from scallions. Cut 2-3-inch pieces from the white end. Using a sharp knife, score the white ends in 1-inch cuts, cutting away from greens. Make 5-8 evenly spaced cuts around each stalk's circumference so that the ends open out. When cut, drop into ice water until ends curl.
